behavior: Perches upright at mid-levels, then sallies or hovers to catch insects in the air or from foliage; also takes spiders and small fruits.
field_marks: Small, upright flycatcher with a relatively large, peaked-looking head
Olive-green upperparts and pale whitish to yellow-washed underparts
Thin, distinct pale eye-ring around a dark eye
Dark wings with two clear pale wingbars and long wing tips
Broad, flat bill: dark above and orange-yellow below
Long, fairly square-tipped tail; dark legs
habitat: Mature deciduous forest and woodland, especially shaded lowlands near streams, rivers, swamps, ravines, or other wet forest; also tropical forest in the nonbreeding season.

sources.title: Acadian Flycatcher Identification — All About Birds
